source.title: The role of the hippocampus in prediction and imagination

confidence_rationale: Buckner 2010 is one of the canonical reframings of hippocampal function away from pure episodic memory retrieval toward prospective prediction and imagination. Very high citation count; synthesises both rodent (preplay/replay) and human (fMRI prospection) literatures. Strong mapping to the claim that hippocampus is a cue-indexed sequence store for future trajectories rather than a passive archive. Transfer risk low.
failure_signatures: Review does not provide direct evidence that hippocampal sequence events are actually used online to guide specific goal-directed choices -- the mechanistic link between hippocampal prospection and action selection remains indirect in the human fMRI literature.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The hippocampal system may be better understood as a prediction/simulation device than a retrieval archive: in rats, ensembles preplay and replay event sequences that, strung in novel combinations, provide building blocks for simulating upcoming events; in humans, the same system supports future event imagination.
mapping.ree_translation: Buckner 2010 provides the conceptual anchor for ARC-007 (hippocampal trajectory store indexed by context) and MECH-033 (kernel-chaining interface). The preplay finding is precisely the claim that hippocampus generates forward trajectories from a stored template library under goal-directed cuing, not from a generative model built on the fly.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The 2010 review predates the Pfeiffer & Foster 2013 goal-directed preplay result, so it is more suggestive than directly evidential. The mechanism by which novel combinations are generated from stored building blocks is not specified.
mapping.source_context: Annual Review of Psychology; synthesis of rodent electrophysiology, human neuroimaging, and patient lesion data.

confidence_components.notes: Landmark review paper; very high prior probability of the claim being correct.
